title: Charmed Life - Note (Fragment 1)
text:
Introduction

Introduction

T
 HERE ARE THOUSANDS of worlds, all different from ours. Chrestomanci’s world is the one next door to us, and the difference here is that magic is as common as music is with us. It is full of people working magic—warlocks, witches, thaumaturges, sorcerers, fakirs, conjurors, hexers, magicians, mages, shamans, diviners and many more—from the lowest Certified witch right up to the most powerful of enchanters. Enchanters are strange as well as powerful. Their magic is different and stronger and many of them have more than one life.

Now, if someone did not control all these busy magic-users, ordinary people would have a horrible time and probably end up as slaves. So the government appoints the very strongest enchanter there is to make sure no one misuses magic. This enchanter has nine lives and is known as “the Chrestomanci.” You pronounce it KREST-OH-MAN-SEE. He has to have a strong personality as well as strong magic.

D IANA W YNNE J ONES

vocabulary.definition: To officially assign a job or role to someone.
vocabulary.example: So the government appoints the very strongest enchanter there is...

vocabulary.definition: Having official recognition or qualification.
vocabulary.example: ...from the lowest Certified witch right up to the most powerful of enchanters.

ru.translation: Хрестоманси (имя собственное, но также ключевой термин во фрагменте)

vocabulary.definition: A unique title referring to the powerful enchanter controlling magic use in the fictional world.
vocabulary.example: The difference here is that magic is as common as music is with us.

vocabulary.definition: Frequently encountered or usual.
vocabulary.example: Magic is as common as music is with us.

vocabulary.definition: Persons who perform magic tricks or summon supernatural forces.
vocabulary.example: ...sorcerers, fakirs, conjurors, hexers, magicians...

vocabulary.definition: People who practice divination or see the future through supernatural means.
vocabulary.example: ...shamans, diviners and many more—from the lowest Certified witch...

vocabulary.definition: People who possess powerful magical abilities and can cast spells.
vocabulary.example: Their magic is different and stronger and many of them have more than one life.

vocabulary.definition: The group of people with the authority to govern a country or state.
vocabulary.example: So the government appoints the very strongest enchanter...

vocabulary.definition: Magic users who cast spells or curses.
vocabulary.example: ...fakirs, conjurors, hexers, magicians, mages...

vocabulary.definition: Very unpleasant or bad.
vocabulary.example: Ordinary people would have a horrible time...

vocabulary.definition: Conditions or modes of existence; in this context, refers to multiple lives the enchanters have.
vocabulary.example: Many of them have more than one life.

vocabulary.definition: Powerful magic users or wizards.
vocabulary.example: ...magicians, mages, shamans, diviners...

vocabulary.definition: People who use magic.
vocabulary.example: If someone did not control all these busy magic-users...

vocabulary.definition: To use something in an incorrect or inappropriate way.
vocabulary.example: So the government appoints the very strongest enchanter to make sure no one misuses magic.

vocabulary.definition: Normal, not special or different.
vocabulary.example: Ordinary people would have a horrible time and probably end up as slaves.

vocabulary.definition: The combination of characteristics or qualities that form an individual's character.
vocabulary.example: He has to have a strong personality as well as strong magic.

vocabulary.definition: Having a lot of strength or influence.
vocabulary.example: ...right up to the most powerful of enchanters.

vocabulary.definition: Almost certainly; as far as one knows or can tell.
vocabulary.example: ...ordinary people would have a horrible time and probably end up as slaves.

vocabulary.definition: Traditional spiritual healers or magic practitioners who mediate between the natural and spirit worlds.
vocabulary.example: ...mages, shamans, diviners and many more...

vocabulary.definition: Unusual or surprising in a way that is unsettling or hard to understand.
vocabulary.example: Enchanters are strange as well as powerful.

vocabulary.definition: Having great physical power or intensity.
vocabulary.example: He has to have a strong personality as well as strong magic.

vocabulary.definition: Practitioners of magic, especially those believed to perform miracles or magic acts.
vocabulary.example: It is full of people working magic—warlocks, witches, thaumaturges, sorcerers...

vocabulary.definition: Male magic users, often associated with witchcraft.
vocabulary.example: It is full of people working magic—warlocks, witches, thaumaturges...
